description: This figure presents a Pearson correlation coefficient matrix heatmap visualizing pairwise correlations among ten input descriptors used for machine learning modeling of low-density FeMnAlC steels. The descriptors include compositional features (Mn, Al, C, Fe) and thermodynamic parameters (VEC, Tm, ΔSmix, ΔHmix, Δχ, δ). Correlation strength is encoded by circle size and color intensity, with blue indicating positive and red indicating negative correlations. Notable strong positive correlations include C with δ (0.98) and Δχ (0.97), as well as δ with Δχ (0.98), while strong negative correlations include Fe with Mn (-0.9), Al with VEC (-0.94), and C with ΔHmix (-0.93). The matrix served to eliminate redundant features prior to model training; features with correlation coefficients exceeding 0.95 (specifically Δχ and δ) were removed to prevent multicollinearity, retaining VEC, Tm, ΔSmix, ΔHmix, and compositional elements for subsequent κ-carbide phase prediction. The visualization thus establishes the feature independence necessary for robust machine learning classification and regression of microstructural outcomes.
